body:
## Summary

- Adds a `formal-ai telegram` CLI subcommand that defaults to Telegram long polling (`getUpdates`) and keeps the existing webhook server available as `--mode=webhook` on the same host/port flags.
- Configures the whole CLI through [`lino-arguments`](https://github.com/link-foundation/lino-arguments) (clap-derive compatible) and calls `lino_arguments::init()` so every flag reads from arguments, environment variables, and `.lenv`/`.env` files.
- Adds `POST /telegram/webhook` to the existing HTTP server, returning direct Telegram `sendMessage` responses for private chats, groups, supergroups, channel posts, and edited message updates.
- Introduces a `TelegramTransport` trait with a curl-backed default (`CurlTelegramTransport`) so the polling loop has no TLS/HTTP-client dependency and is fully unit-tested through a scripted transport.
- Adds execution reports to hello-world answers, including check/run commands, captured output, and an explicit TypeScript unavailable status when `tsc` is not configured.
- Keeps the web demo in parity with the Rust engine by showing the same execution metadata in worker answers.
- Documents issue #8 requirements (R31-R47), the iteration on long polling, online research, solution options, local tool limitations, and verification evidence under `docs/case-studies/issue-8`.

## How to Run

```bash
# Long polling (default), reading TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from env or .lenv/.env:
cargo run -- telegram

# Webhook server (opt-in):
cargo run -- telegram --mode webhook --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8080
```

The published `formal-ai` crate exposes the same `telegram` subcommand through `cargo install formal-ai`, so the Telegram CLI is shipped from a Cargo crate as requested.

## Reproduction and Tests

The original failing behavior was reproduced with unit tests: `/telegram/webhook` returned 404, and Rust hello-world answers had no execution status or captured output.

Automated coverage now includes:

- Webhook handler: `telegram_webhook_supports_private_messages`, `telegram_webhook_supports_public_chat_code_replies`.
- Hello-world execution-output assertions for Rust, Python, JavaScript, and Go.
- Polling configuration: `polling_config_builds_get_updates_url_with_offset`, `polling_config_omits_offset_when_unset`, `url_encoding_uses_percent_for_reserved_characters`, `json_array_encoding_quotes_values`.
- Polling parser: `parse_returns_replies_and_next_offset`, `parse_returns_unexpected_response_when_ok_false`, `parse_skips_updates_without_message_payload`.
- Polling runtime: `polling_loop_sends_replies_and_advances_offset`, `polling_loop_recovers_from_invalid_payload` (both using a scripted transport).
- Playwright coverage that the web demo still renders the Rust execution status.

Local verification run (all green):

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo test --all-features --verbose` (9 lib + 2 integration suites + 40 webhook/demo tests)
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo run --quiet -- telegram --help` (verifies flag wiring, env, and `.lenv`/`.env` integration through `lino_arguments::init()`)
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `node --check docs/demo/formal_ai_worker.js`
- `git diff --check`

## Requirements Coverage

| Source | Requirement | Where |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Issue #8 | Provide a Telegram bot. | `formal-ai telegram` (polling default) and `POST /telegram/webhook`. |
| Issue #8 | Support private and public chats. | Unit tests for private and supergroup-style chat IDs. |
| Issue #8 | Compile/run code blocks and show output. | Hello-world seeds verified with `start-command`. |
| Issue #8 | Report environment limits. | TypeScript answers say `tsc` is unavailable. |
| PR #9 follow-up comment | Run the bot in CLI with polling by default, keep webhook as an option. | `--mode polling` is the default; `--mode webhook` reuses the existing server. |
| PR #9 follow-up comment | Publish the Telegram CLI in a Cargo crate. | Same published `formal-ai` crate; the `telegram` subcommand ships with `cargo install`. |
| PR #9 follow-up comment | Use lino-arguments with clap-style config. | `lino_arguments::Parser` derive plus `lino_arguments::init()` in `main`. |

## Notes

`start-command` is available locally and was smoke-tested. Docker and `tsc` are not installed in the prepared runtime, so Docker-backed isolation was not used and TypeScript answers report that limitation instead of claiming execution.

The Telegram webhook path is intentionally stateless and does not store a bot token. The polling path expects a token through `--token` or `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`. Short replies work through direct webhook responses or polling `sendMessage` calls; large output attachments would still need an outbound bot-client layer that can call `sendDocument` with credentials and multipart upload.

The polling client shells out to `curl`, so the runtime must have `curl` installed. This trade-off keeps the dependency graph small and lets the polling loop stay synchronous and unit-tested through the `TelegramTransport` trait.

Fixes #8

## Working session summary

Implemented issue #8 and updated PR 9: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/9

What changed:
- Added `POST /telegram/webhook` with direct Telegram `sendMessage` replies.
- Added Telegram private/public chat unit coverage.
- Added execution metadata/output to hello-world answers.
- Updated the web demo worker to show the same execution metadata.
- Added issue-8 requirements, case-study data, research notes, logs, and changelog fragment.

Verification:
- Local checks passed, including `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo test`, doc tests, file-size/changelog checks, `node --check`, and Playwright E2E.
- CI passed on commit `75282af`: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/25757909592
- PR is marked ready for review.
- Working tree is clean.

Noted boundary: Docker and `tsc` were unavailable in the prepared runtime, so TypeScript is explicitly reported as not verified, and Docker-backed isolation was not used. Research sources are documented under `docs/case-studies/issue-8`, including Telegram Bot API, `teloxide`, `frankenstein`, and `link-foundation/start`.

## Working session summary

All work is done. Final summary:

**PR #9** ([link](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/9)) — "Add Telegram bot interface with polling CLI and webhook"

- New `formal-ai telegram` subcommand: long polling default, `--mode=webhook` opt-in
- CLI uses `lino_arguments::Parser` + `lino_arguments::init()` (clap-style, env + `.lenv`/`.env`)
- `TelegramTransport` trait with curl-backed default — no TLS dep, fully unit-tested via scripted transport
- 9 lib tests + 40 integration tests, all CI jobs green on `a953328` (lint, format, fragment check, version check, code coverage, ubuntu/macos/windows test, build, E2E local demo)
- Docs updated: README, REQUIREMENTS.md (R42-R47), `docs/case-studies/issue-8/README.md`, changelog fragment with `bump: minor`
- Main merged into branch, PR moved out of draft

title: Add Telegram bot interface with polling CLI and webhook
